reflection:
Three days ago the WHOOP clocked you at 49% recovery and you got 4 hours of sleep through pain in every position. Yesterday: recovery 71, 7.3 hours, anxiety zero. The body isn't broken. It recovered fast.

The 3am wake is worth tracking. Toilet more than usual, erection not as strong as normal. Amitriptyline was paused April 14 - that's only 5 days of clearance. The sexual side effect is a known part of the washout, not a new problem starting. Give it another week before drawing conclusions.

What the data actually shows: HRV was 38.8ms yesterday, still a bit below your baseline. System is still recalibrating. That morning tiredness, energy 6 while mood was 8, that's the gap. Body is working hard behind the scenes.

Today you have Michael Parr at 08:30. Six hours with someone trained in Perrin Technique and DNS for central sensitization. The one thing worth doing: tell him about April 16 in detail. The cold turkey stop, the 2am wake, pain 9, heat-cold swings, all of it. He needs to understand what your nervous system just went through to calibrate his approach.

The London week is here.
opener: Pain 9 to 4 in 3 days. Recovery is real. Today is Michael Parr.
focus_for_today: Tell Michael Parr about the April 16 crash in full detail - that context shapes everything.
